Get Your Solar Proposal

Sun Direct Renewable - Alberta

Solar Panel Installation in Calgary

Calgary’s bright climate creates meaningful solar potential, but the right system still depends on the property, electrical use and local approval process.

Home/Solar Panel Installation in Calgary

Solar for Calgary properties

Homes, commercial buildings and rural properties near Calgary each bring different roof, land, usage and interconnection considerations.

Local process, clearly managed

A complete project can include energy review, site assessment, design, permits, interconnection steps, installation, commissioning and monitoring.

Winter and micro-generation

Cold sunny weather can support panel performance, while annual modelling accounts for shorter days, weather and snow. Export arrangements should be confirmed for your account.

Frequently asked questions

What to know about solar

Will solar eliminate my electricity bill completely?+

Not necessarily. Your result depends on electricity use, system size, seasonal production, utility charges and export credits. A proposal should show realistic bill-offset assumptions instead of promising a zero bill.

Do solar panels still produce electricity during an Alberta winter?+

Yes. Panels use daylight rather than heat and can operate efficiently in cold conditions. Shorter days, snow cover, shading and weather still affect seasonal production, so annual modelling matters.

Will my solar panels keep the power on during an outage?+

A standard grid-connected system normally shuts down during an outage for safety. Backup power generally requires compatible battery equipment and system design specifically intended for backup operation.

What if my roof needs to be replaced in a few years?+

Roof age and condition should be reviewed before installation. If replacement is likely soon, it may be more practical to complete the roofing work first rather than pay to remove and reinstall the array later.

What happens when my panels produce more electricity than I am using?+

An approved micro-generation system may export unused electricity to the grid. The credit structure depends on your retailer, utility arrangements and current terms for the property.

What should I have ready before requesting a solar proposal?+

A recent electricity bill, ideally twelve months of usage, the property address and any plans for future loads such as an EV or new equipment give the assessment a stronger starting point.

Do I need permits or utility approval before solar can be installed?+

Most grid-connected projects involve utility interconnection and may require permits or other approvals. The exact requirements depend on the property, municipality, electrical service and proposed system design.

How long does a solar project usually take?+

Timing depends on the site review, system design, equipment availability, utility requirements, permits, weather and inspection scheduling. A property-specific proposal can outline the steps and the factors that may affect the timeline.

How much maintenance do solar panels need?+

Solar systems are generally low maintenance, but monitoring, occasional visual checks and access after severe weather remain important. Your handover should explain the selected equipment, monitoring platform and recommended support process.

Your next step

Turn Sunlight Into a Smarter Energy Plan

Share your electricity usage and property details. We’ll organize the information needed to shape a clear solar proposal.
